required_complexity_patterns.notes: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute
At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element
Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers
When combobox widget requested, use custom listbox - native select alone is insufficient
Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute Required: At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element Required: Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers Required: When combobox widget requested, use custom listbox - native select alone is insufficient Required: Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input Reference DOM (Other option reveal): Referral source Other checkbox reveals free-text input Reference DOM (Aria-labelledby only): Email field identified only via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden span Reference DOM (Collapsible section animation): Additional questions in collapsible section toggled by aria-expanded button Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: label-wraps-deep, aria-label-only, split-phone, native-select-not-combobox, div-role-textbox. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: sidebar + main split apply layout. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Other option reveal; Aria-labelledby only; Collapsible section animation.
